top_openings.name: Unknown Opening
top_openings.reason: This opening appears in your games and may be worth testing further.
top_openings.plan: Focus on development, king safety and understanding the first pawn break.
top_openings.mistakeToAvoid: Do not judge it from one game. Build a small sample before deciding.

top_openings.name: Sicilian Defence
top_openings.reason: Creates unbalanced games with lots of winning chances.
top_openings.plan: Fight for the centre from the side and look for counterplay instead of copying White.
top_openings.mistakeToAvoid: Do not ignore development while chasing tactics.

top_openings.name: Modern Defence
top_openings.reason: This opening appears in your games and may be worth testing further.
top_openings.plan: Focus on development, king safety and understanding the first pawn break.
top_openings.mistakeToAvoid: Do not judge it from one game. Build a small sample before deciding.

top_openings.name: French Defence
top_openings.reason: Fits solid players who like structure and counterattacking pawn breaks.
top_openings.plan: Challenge White's centre with ...c5 and develop with a clear pawn structure.
top_openings.mistakeToAvoid: Do not trap your light-squared bishop forever.

top_openings.name: English Opening
top_openings.reason: Fits flexible players who like choosing the structure after seeing the opponent's setup.
top_openings.plan: Control the centre from the side and transpose into comfortable structures.
top_openings.mistakeToAvoid: Do not become too slow or vague with your development.

top_openings.name: Réti Opening
top_openings.reason: Fits flexible players who like choosing the structure after seeing the opponent's setup.
top_openings.plan: Control the centre from the side and transpose into comfortable structures.
top_openings.mistakeToAvoid: Do not become too slow or vague with your development.

top_openings.name: Caro-Kann Defence
top_openings.reason: Good if you want a solid structure with fewer early traps.
top_openings.plan: Build a strong centre, develop safely and counterattack later.
top_openings.mistakeToAvoid: Do not play too passively after completing development.
top_openings.difficulty: Easy to learn, harder to master

top_openings.name: Queen Pawn Game
top_openings.reason: Good for players who like stable centres and long-term pressure.
top_openings.plan: Control the centre, develop calmly and prepare pressure on the queenside or centre.
top_openings.mistakeToAvoid: Do not grab pawns if it slows your development too much.

recommendations: As White, your most common practical choice is Unknown Opening.
As Black, your most common structure is Unknown Opening.
Your best-scoring recurring opening is Caro-Kann Defence.
You look suited to openings with clear structure and repeatable plans.
Your results suggest tactics and initiative are worth leaning into.

style_profile.summary: You often choose stable structures and clear development. Your games suggest you are comfortable in sharper positions. You seem comfortable transposing between different setups.

styleProfile.summary: You often choose stable structures and clear development. Your games suggest you are comfortable in sharper positions. You seem comfortable transposing between different setups.

whiteDetailed.name: London System
whiteDetailed.reason: Reliable for players who want repeatable development and low theory.
whiteDetailed.plan: Set up Bf4, e3, Nf3, Bd3 and castle before choosing a central break.
whiteDetailed.mistakeToAvoid: Do not play the same setup without reacting to your opponent.

whiteDetailed.name: Queen's Gambit
whiteDetailed.reason: Good for players who like stable centres and long-term pressure.
whiteDetailed.plan: Control the centre, develop calmly and prepare pressure on the queenside or centre.
whiteDetailed.mistakeToAvoid: Do not grab pawns if it slows your development too much.

whiteDetailed.name: Italian Game
whiteDetailed.reason: Gives simple development, quick castling and clear attacking ideas.
whiteDetailed.plan: Develop pieces quickly, castle early and build pressure in the centre.
whiteDetailed.mistakeToAvoid: Avoid moving the same piece repeatedly in the opening.

whiteDetailed.name: Vienna Game
whiteDetailed.reason: Fits players who like quick development, attacking chances and natural piece play.
whiteDetailed.plan: Develop with Nc3, Bc4 and aim for pressure on the centre or f7.
whiteDetailed.mistakeToAvoid: Do not attack too early before your king is safe.

whiteDetailed.name: Scotch Game
whiteDetailed.reason: Creates open positions quickly, which suits tactical players.
whiteDetailed.plan: Open the centre early and use active piece development to create threats.
whiteDetailed.mistakeToAvoid: Do not trade into positions where your pieces become passive.

blackDetailed.name: Caro-Kann Defence
blackDetailed.reason: Good if you want a solid structure with fewer early traps.
blackDetailed.plan: Build a strong centre, develop safely and counterattack later.
blackDetailed.mistakeToAvoid: Do not play too passively after completing development.
blackDetailed.difficulty: Easy to learn, harder to master

blackDetailed.name: French Defence
blackDetailed.reason: Fits solid players who like structure and counterattacking pawn breaks.
blackDetailed.plan: Challenge White's centre with ...c5 and develop with a clear pawn structure.
blackDetailed.mistakeToAvoid: Do not trap your light-squared bishop forever.

blackDetailed.name: Queen's Gambit Declined ideas
blackDetailed.reason: Good for players who like stable centres and long-term pressure.
blackDetailed.plan: Control the centre, develop calmly and prepare pressure on the queenside or centre.
blackDetailed.mistakeToAvoid: Do not grab pawns if it slows your development too much.

blackDetailed.name: Sicilian Defence
blackDetailed.reason: Creates unbalanced games with lots of winning chances.
blackDetailed.plan: Fight for the centre from the side and look for counterplay instead of copying White.
blackDetailed.mistakeToAvoid: Do not ignore development while chasing tactics.

blackDetailed.name: Scandinavian Defence
blackDetailed.reason: Good for players who want direct positions and simple development plans.
blackDetailed.plan: Challenge the centre early, develop quickly and avoid wasting queen moves.
blackDetailed.mistakeToAvoid: Do not move the queen too many times in the opening.

training_plan: As White, play Unknown Opening for your next 10 to 15 games.
As Black, stick with Unknown Opening and learn the first 6 to 8 moves well.
Keep building around Caro-Kann Defence. It is currently your best practical opening.
Focus on development, king safety and reaching familiar pawn structures every game.
After each game, review where tactics first appeared rather than only the final blunder.
Choose one main line and one backup line so flexibility does not become inconsistency.
